---
title: "about me"
subtitle: "my data science portfolio"
---

Hi, I'm Alcuin Abraham, a Data Science major at Brigham Young University - Idaho with a strong interest in building practical systems that help teams work more efficiently and make better decisions.

I enjoy work that sits at the intersection of data, automation, and problem solving. Recent projects and roles have included building AI-assisted internal tools, improving product research workflows, and developing automated data pipelines for university relations. I am especially drawn to problems where good analysis and thoughtful systems design can remove friction and create lasting improvements.

I tend to approach work with initiative, curiosity, and a willingness to learn quickly. Whether I am working on a technical project, improving an internal process, or helping a team get aligned, I care about building solutions that are scalable, reliable, and genuinely useful.

## What you'll find here

This portfolio is a place to share the projects, experiments, and skills I am developing across data science, storytelling, machine learning, and full stack work. It reflects both what I am learning and how I like to think through real problems.

## A little more about me

Beyond coursework and technical projects, I value teamwork, clear communication, and steady growth. My volunteer and professional experience has taught me a lot about leadership, training, and creating systems that support other people well.
